title: Risk-Normalized Position Sizing and Realistic Cost Diagnosis

pre_implementation_diagnosis.entry_and_signal_timing: Signals and entries are unchanged. A BUY enters at the signal candle close and exits cannot occur before the next candle.
pre_implementation_diagnosis.baseline_sizing: The stable baseline allocates 100% of current balance to every trade. Quantity is implicit as allocated capital divided by entry price.
pre_implementation_diagnosis.stop_availability: Fixed-percentage and ATR stop prices are both known before entry. ATR continues to use only the previous completed candle.
pre_implementation_diagnosis.leverage: The baseline is full cash allocation without leverage. The new variant explicitly rejects leverage and caps notional at available cash.
pre_implementation_diagnosis.fees: BacktestEngine already supports a fee percentage on both entry and exit, but the generated research grid passes zero fees.
pre_implementation_diagnosis.slippage: Historical backtests do not model entry or exit slippage. Monte Carlo perturbs returns later, which is not a substitute for execution-price slippage.
pre_implementation_diagnosis.scope_decision: Only position sizing was implemented. Adding historical slippage safely requires a separate execution-price contract and cap-inclusive fee treatment, so costs were deferred rather than added approximately.

implementation.formula: quantity = min((balance * risk_fraction) / stop_distance, (balance * allocation_cap) / entry_price, balance / entry_price)
implementation.invalid_stop_policy: A missing, non-finite, non-positive, or non-long stop distance cannot open a risk-normalized trade.
implementation.compounding: Risk budget and allocation cap are recalculated from closed-trade balance before each entry.
implementation.baseline_preservation: Strategies without position_sizing_mode=risk_normalized retain the original full-allocation calculations.
implementation.candidate_policy: The original 30 fixed-exit and 30 ATR generated candidates remain first and unchanged. Sixty deterministic risk variants follow with unique IDs.
implementation.validation_policy: All progressive-funnel, robustness, Monte Carlo, and final-readiness thresholds remain unchanged.

reporting_contract.return_semantics: Raw PnL % is the unscaled price return. PnL % is the account return after sizing and fees. Monte Carlo receives the account return and sized monetary PnL.
reporting_contract.edge_claim_policy: Raw metrics are reported separately so exposure reduction is not described as improved strategy edge.

paired_exposure_results.selection_confound: Sixteen risk variants selected a different SL/TP grid winner because the existing overall score includes exposure-sensitive ROI and drawdown. Their raw-edge differences are not attributed to sizing. The stable selector was not redesigned in this milestone.

risk_cap_evidence.interpretation: The 25% allocation cap bound every tested risk trade. Actual stop risk stayed below the 1% budget, from 0.1875% to 0.75% of equity, so no trade exceeded the declared risk ceiling.

robustness_and_monte_carlo_impact.interpretation: Risk normalization materially improved drawdown and Monte Carlo stability for several candidates, allowing four risk variants to reach PROMISING_REVIEW. It did not repair profit factor, walk-forward consistency, or regime breadth.

data_quality.coverage_status: All five pairs have approximately one year of complete 15m data with no estimated missing candles, duplicates, or invalid rows.

paper_trading_readiness.reason: All six shortlisted candidates still miss at least one unchanged production gate. The dominant failures are profit factor below 1.20, walk-forward pass rate below 0.60, and limited profitable-regime coverage.

remaining_risks: Historical fees and slippage remain zero, so absolute returns and profit factors are optimistic.
The 25% allocation cap binds before the 1% risk budget for every tested stop, making this variant conservative but preventing a pure 1%-risk comparison.
Sixteen selected grid winners differ between baseline and risk variants because the existing ranking score is exposure-sensitive.
ATR candidates retain negative average raw expectancy and high trade counts; sizing limits damage but does not create edge.
The strongest candidates still lack sufficient walk-forward and regime consistency for Paper Trading.

precise_follow_up_cost_plan.scope: A separate predeclared cost-model milestone, without changing strategy signals, sizing, or validation gates.
precise_follow_up_cost_plan.configuration: Add per-side Binance Spot fee and adverse slippage percentages to research configuration, with explicit zero-cost baseline variants retained.
precise_follow_up_cost_plan.execution: Apply adverse slippage to entry and exit execution prices before PnL; apply fees to both notional legs; include entry fee in the no-leverage cash cap.
precise_follow_up_cost_plan.validation: Unit-test long entry/stop/target/forced-exit price directions, fee arithmetic, cap compliance, deterministic results, and Monte Carlo handoff; then rerun the same benchmark groups.
precise_follow_up_cost_plan.promotion_rule: Do not promote any candidate whose post-cost raw profit factor, walk-forward result, robustness status, or final readiness fails the existing thresholds.

conclusion: Phase 21.3 is a stable risk-control improvement, not an edge improvement. It reduces exposure and drawdown without weakening validation, produces four additional promising-review candidates, and leaves Paper Trading correctly NOT_READY.
